target.title: fix(tui): wire Crestodian /audit /doctor /health /setup slash command…
target.body:
Summary
Two TUI chat bugs fixed:

Crestodian shortcuts unreachable as slash commands. The Crestodian planner advertises audit, doctor, health, setup (and similar) as top-level commands, but the TUI chat had no matching slash entries, and parseCrestodianOperation("/audit") rejected the leading slash because of lower === "audit" style checks. Typing /audit, /doctor, /health, or /setup either fell through to sendMessage(raw) (sent verbatim to the agent) or got dragged through the LLM planner for nothing.
Node 25 "Detected unsettled top-level await" warning scribbled over the chat input prompt. The literal top-level await chain in openclaw.mjs triggers Node 25's TLA detector when the launched runtime (chat TUI / gateway) is interrupted, and the warning text + source frame overwrite the readline prompt.
What changed
src/tui/commands.ts: adds /audit, /doctor, /health, /setup to getSlashCommands() and helpText() so they show up in autocomplete and /help.
src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts: routes the four new commands through the existing Crestodian handoff (requestExit({ exitReason: "return-to-crestodian", crestodianMessage })) when running against a normal agent backend, and through sendMessage(...) when already inside the Crestodian backend (detected via the new CRESTODIAN_TUI_CONNECTION_URL). Extra args are forwarded, e.g. /doctor fix -> crestodianMessage: "doctor fix".
src/crestodian/operations.ts: parseCrestodianOperation now strips a leading / from the trimmed input as defense-in-depth, so any slash-prefixed Crestodian command parses identically to its natural-language form.
src/crestodian/tui-connection-id.ts (new): tiny dependency-free file exporting CRESTODIAN_TUI_CONNECTION_URL = "crestodian local". Imported by both src/crestodian/tui-backend.ts and src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts to avoid forming a cycle through ../tui/tui.js.
openclaw.mjs: wraps the bootstrap main flow in an async IIFE (void (async () => { ... })().catch(...)) so the module no longer has any top-level await for Node 25 to flag. respawnWithoutCompileCacheIfNeeded(), module.enableCompileCache(), and ensureSupportedNodeVersion() stay synchronous.
src/infra/warning-filter.ts: defensive clause for any message.includes("Detected unsettled top-level await") so older installs still suppress the warning once the entry runs.

Tests
New / extended:

src/tui/commands.test.ts - getSlashCommands() includes the four new entries with the expected descriptions; helpText() lists /audit, /doctor, /health, /setup.
src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts - extends the harness with a connection.url and adds three cases: /audit against a non-Crestodian backend triggers requestExit({ exitReason: "return-to-crestodian", crestodianMessage: "audit" }); /doctor fix forwards the args; /audit against the Crestodian backend (connection.url === "crestodian local") calls sendChat({ message: "audit", ... }) and does not request exit.
src/crestodian/operations.test.ts - parseCrestodianOperation accepts /audit, /doctor, /doctor fix, /health, /setup, /status.
src/infra/warning-filter.test.ts - shouldIgnoreWarning matches the "Detected unsettled top-level await" message; the installProcessWarningFilter integration test verifies the synthetic warning emit never reaches process.on("warning").
Test plan

 pnpm exec oxfmt --check on every touched file - clean

 pnpm tsgo:core - clean

 pnpm tsgo:core:test - clean

 pnpm lint:core - 0 warnings, 0 errors across 7,830 files

 pnpm check:import-cycles - 0 runtime cycles

 pnpm check:madge-import-cycles - 0 cycles

 Targeted vitest: 32 TUI tests pass + 3 warning-filter tests pass + 13/14 operations tests pass (the one remaining failure is a pre-existing Windows path-normalization issue on main in parseCrestodianOperation > runs setup bootstrap only after approval and audits it, unrelated to this PR; confirmed by stashing the parser change and reproducing the same failure)

 node --check openclaw.mjs passes; node openclaw.mjs --help boots end-to-end through the new IIFE and prints the OpenClaw help banner

 pnpm build tsdown step + check-cli-bootstrap-imports pass (the runtime-postbuild stage-bundled-plugin-runtime-deps step fails only because Cursor's bundled Node lacks an npm binary on Windows - environmental, not caused by this PR)

 pnpm check:changed and full pnpm test on Testbox before merge (recommended on a maintainer machine since openclaw.mjs touches the publishable surface)
Why this is the right fix
Bug A is fixed in the owner modules (src/crestodian/* for parser and connection identifier, src/tui/* for the slash-command surface), with no behavior change to existing slash commands. The new commands reuse the existing /crestodian <message> handoff path that ships today, so we don't introduce a new exit path or dialog state.
Bug B is fixed at the source by removing the actual top-level await from the published bootstrap script (the only true fix for installed binaries), and the warning-filter clause covers older installs and any future regression. The IIFE preserves all observable behavior, including the missing-dist/entry error message - verified by running node openclaw.mjs --help against a built tree and against an unbuilt tree.
